recommended_sampling.notes: Google specifies these values as standardized across ALL use cases — including coding, which is unusual (most models prefer lower temp for code). Sampler order matters for llama.cpp: temperature → top_p → top_k. min_p and repetition_penalty not documented by Google; leave unset.

recommended_context.yarn_notes: Native 256K (262144-token) context trained in. No YaRN/RoPE extension needed within that limit. Global attention layers use Proportional RoPE (p-RoPE) for memory efficiency at long context. Sliding-window local layers use 1024-token windows; KV for those layers does NOT grow with sequence length, reducing VRAM pressure for long contexts. For llama.cpp: use -c with values up to 262144; expect high KV cache usage at full context for global layers.

chat_template.notes:
Gemma 4 uses NEW token-style different from Gemma 3. Gemma 3 used <start_of_turn>/<end_of_turn>; Gemma 4 uses <|turn>role
 … <turn|>
 markers. System role is NATIVELY supported in Gemma 4 (breaking change vs Gemma 3, which folded system into first user turn). Template file: google/gemma-4-31B-it/chat_template.jinja. Format: <|turn>system
{system}<turn|>
<|turn>user
{user}<turn|>
<|turn>model
. Special thinking tokens: enable with <|think|> at start of system prompt; thinking output wrapped in <|channel>thought
{reasoning}
<channel|>. For llama.cpp/llama-server: --chat-template-kwargs '{"enable_thinking":false}' to disable thinking; Windows PowerShell: use escaped quotes. Always pull the embedded jinja from the GGUF or use the HF template — early GGUFs (pre May 2026) had broken templates and required re-download.

thinking.how: Add <|think|> token at the start of the system prompt to enable. Reasoning output appears as <|channel>thought\n[reasoning]\n<channel|> before the final answer. In multi-turn: strip thinking blocks from history — only keep final visible answer before the next user turn.
thinking.recommended: Optional. Disable for routine tasks (--chat-template-kwargs '{"enable_thinking":false}'); enable for complex reasoning. When disabled, larger models may still emit empty thought blocks before responses — this is expected.

llama_cpp_notes: Sliding-window hybrid attention: 60 layers total; local SWA layers use 1024-token window (KV fixed size), global attention layers use full context (KV grows). Final layer is always global. For long-context (>32K) use --cache-type-k f16 or q8_0 for better quality. QAT variant (lmstudio-community/gemma-4-31B-it-QAT-GGUF) is trained at Q4_0 precision by Google — near-BF16 quality at 4-bit, ~3x memory reduction vs BF16. IMPORTANT: naive Q4_0 conversion degrades quality because llama.cpp Q4_0 uses F16 scales while Google's QAT used BF16 scales during training; prefer the lmstudio-community or unsloth UD-Q4_K_XL dynamic GGUFs which correctly handle this. The QAT GGUF from lmstudio-community is the purpose-built variant. Multimodal: requires --mmproj flag for vision encoder (~550M params); text-only inference works without it. Model parameters: 30.7B active, 32.7B total (includes ~550M vision encoder).

finetune_overrides.notes: QAT (Quantization-Aware Training) variant. Google trained this specifically at Q4_0 precision — near-BF16 quality at ~18GB. Do NOT re-quantize; use as-is. Sampling params are identical to standard variant (temp=1.0, top_p=0.95, top_k=64). This is the recommended local deployment variant for 24GB VRAM (RTX 4090).
finetune_overrides.memory_note: ~18GB VRAM for weights; add KV cache on top. Full 256K context requires significant additional VRAM for global attention layers.

sources.label: Unsloth GGUF repo — inference params and quantization options
Gemma 4 QAT self-hosting guide — QAT quality and llama.cpp scale mismatch warning
Ollama gemma4:31b-it-qat — QAT variant specs (Q4_0, 256K, 60 layers, 1024 SWA)
Google AI Gemma prompt structure docs (Gemma 3/4 template reference)

gaps: 1. Exact global vs local layer count ratio not confirmed from first-party source (60 total layers known, split unknown). 2. min_p not documented by Google — likely 0.0 (unset) but unverified. 3. repetition_penalty not documented. 4. The ai.google.dev prompt-structure page still describes Gemma 3-style (no system role, start_of_turn/end_of_turn); it may be outdated for Gemma 4 — the HF model card and Jinja template confirm native system role and new token style. 5. Audio modality confirmed unsupported in 31B (only E2B/E4B have audio). 6. LM Studio community GGUF chat_template patch status (pre/post May 2026 re-download) not verified for these specific local files.
researched_by: claude-sonnet via HF lookup + WebFetch + WebSearch
